title: Password Reset Procedure
description: User cannot log in, forgot password, or account is locked out
steps: Verify user identity via employee ID and registered email.
Navigate to Active Directory → Users → Locate the account.
Click 'Reset Password' and generate a temporary secure password.
Ensure 'User must change password at next logon' is checked.
Notify user via registered email with the temporary password.
Confirm MFA (multi-factor authentication) is still active after reset.
Log resolution in ticket system with timestamp.
result: Password successfully reset. User can now log in.

title: VPN Connection Failure
description: User cannot connect to VPN, VPN client error, remote access issue
steps: Check if the VPN client is up-to-date — update if not.
Verify the user is connecting to the correct VPN server address.
Ask the user to disable any third-party firewall/antivirus temporarily.
Run the VPN client as Administrator.
Clear VPN client cache: go to Settings → Clear Saved Sessions.
Check VPN gateway availability from the network admin portal.
If issue persists, re-install the VPN client from the IT software portal.

title: Blue Screen of Death (BSOD) / System Crash
description: Computer shows blue screen, crashes, kernel panic, stop error
steps: Note the stop code displayed on the BSOD screen (e.g., MEMORY_MANAGEMENT).
Restart the machine and boot into Safe Mode.
Open Event Viewer → Windows Logs → System — check for critical errors.
Run Windows Memory Diagnostic Tool (mdsched.exe) to test RAM.
Run sfc /scannow in an elevated Command Prompt to repair system files.
Check for recent driver updates and roll back any new drivers.
If stop code relates to disk: run chkdsk /f /r on the system drive.
Escalate to hardware team if RAM or disk failure is confirmed.
result: Root cause identified; follow-up action required.
escalationReason: Possible hardware failure — L2 hardware team review needed.

title: Software Installation Request
description: User needs to install software, application not available, software request
steps: Verify software is listed in the approved IT software catalog.
Check if the user has a valid license or license is available in the pool.
Approve or deny request via the IT request management portal.
If approved, push installation via SCCM/Intune to the user's device.
Notify user when installation is complete.
Update the CMDB with the new software installation record.
result: Software installation request submitted for approval.
escalationReason: Requires IT manager approval for non-standard software.

title: Cannot Access Shared Network Drive
description: Network drive not mapped, cannot access shared folder, file server unreachable
steps: Verify user is connected to the corporate network or VPN.
Check that the network drive mapping exists: open File Explorer → This PC.
Re-map the drive: right-click This PC → Map Network Drive → enter the UNC path.
Confirm the user has permissions in Active Directory for the shared folder.
Test connectivity to the file server: ping <server-name>.
Check file server status on the infrastructure dashboard.
Submit firewall rule review request if access is still denied.

title: Email Not Syncing / Outlook Issues
description: Outlook not sending or receiving emails, email sync issues, OST file corrupt
steps: Check user's internet and Exchange server connectivity.
Restart Outlook and verify the account is connected (bottom status bar).
Run Outlook in safe mode: Win+R → outlook /safe.
Rebuild the Outlook profile: Control Panel → Mail → Show Profiles → Add.
If OST file is corrupted, run scanpst.exe (Inbox Repair Tool).
Verify Exchange server status from the admin portal.
Re-activate Microsoft 365 license if activation error appears.

title: Printer Not Working
description: Printer offline, cannot print, print spooler error, printer not found
steps: Check printer power and physical cable/wireless connection.
Set the printer as the default printer: Settings → Printers & Scanners.
Clear the print queue: Services → Print Spooler → Stop → delete files in C:\Windows\System32\spool\PRINTERS → Restart spooler.
Remove and re-add the printer from Printers & Scanners settings.
Re-install printer drivers from the manufacturer or IT software portal.
Test printing a test page from the printer's own display.
If network printer, verify the printer IP is reachable via ping.

title: Suspected Malware / Virus Infection
description: Malware detected, ransomware, suspicious files, antivirus alert, computer infected
steps: IMMEDIATELY disconnect the device from the corporate network (unplug LAN / disable Wi-Fi).
Do NOT shut the machine down — preserve volatile memory for forensics.
Notify the Security Operations Center (SOC) immediately via phone.
Open the endpoint security console to isolate the machine remotely.
Capture a memory dump and disk image for forensic analysis.
Initiate the Incident Response runbook (IR-SEC-001).
Reset all credentials the machine had access to from a clean device.
result: Security incident initiated. Awaiting SOC response.
escalationReason: Security incident — mandatory SOC and management escalation.

title: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Setup / Reset
description: User lost MFA device, cannot receive OTP, MFA authenticator app not working, new phone setup
steps: Verify user identity via HR department or manager confirmation.
Admin: sign in to Azure AD / Okta → Select user → Authentication methods.
Delete the existing MFA methods for the user.
Send the user a fresh MFA registration link via a secondary verified channel.
Guide the user to re-enroll the Authenticator app on the new device.
Test login with the new MFA setup before closing the ticket.
Log the MFA reset event in the security audit trail.
escalationReason: Requires identity verification — cannot be fully automated.

title: Internet / Network Connectivity Issues
description: No internet access, slow internet, DNS failure, IP conflict
steps: Run ipconfig /all to check IP configuration.
Try ipconfig /release followed by ipconfig /renew to get a fresh IP.
Flush DNS cache: ipconfig /flushdns.
Test connectivity: ping 8.8.8.8 (Google DNS) and ping google.com.
If DNS fails but IP works, set DNS to 8.8.8.8 manually.
Check network adapter settings for IP conflicts.
Restart the network adapter: Device Manager → Network Adapters → Disable/Enable.
Escalate to network team if the issue affects multiple users.

title: Computer Running Slow / High CPU Usage
description: Laptop is very slow, high CPU or memory usage, applications freezing
steps: Open Task Manager (Ctrl+Shift+Esc) → identify processes consuming high CPU/memory.
End non-essential high-usage processes.
Check startup programs: Task Manager → Startup tab → Disable unnecessary items.
Run Disk Cleanup and clear temporary files.
Check for pending Windows Updates — install and restart.
Run Malwarebytes scan to rule out malware.
If RAM is below 8GB, submit a hardware upgrade request via the IT portal.
If SSD is full, archive old files to network storage.
result: Performance improved. Long-term: hardware review recommended.

title: Application Crashing or Not Opening
description: Application keeps crashing, software not responding, app fails to launch
steps: Check Event Viewer → Application logs for the specific crash error.
Run the application as Administrator.
Clear application cache / temp files.
Uninstall and re-install the application from the IT software portal.
Check if the application is compatible with the current OS version.
Verify that required dependencies (e.g., .NET, Visual C++ Redistributable) are installed.
Submit a bug report to the software vendor if issue persists.

title: New Employee Onboarding — Account Setup
description: New employee account creation, new user setup, create AD account, onboarding
steps: Receive approved onboarding form from HR with employee details.
Create Active Directory account with correct OU, department, and manager.
Assign Microsoft 365 license group based on job role.
Add user to appropriate security groups and distribution lists.
Provision email account and send welcome email with credentials.
Configure MFA registration and send enrollment link.
Set up laptop via Autopilot or image deployment with user profile.
Brief user on IT policies and provide IT helpdesk contact.
result: New user account created and device provisioned.
escalationReason: Requires HR authorization and IT manager approval.

title: Data Recovery / Deleted File Recovery
description: Accidentally deleted files, need to recover data, files missing
steps: Check the Recycle Bin first — restore if file is there.
Check OneDrive / SharePoint version history for cloud-synced files.
On file servers: right-click the folder → Properties → Previous Versions to restore from shadow copy.
If files were on a local drive, use Windows File History if configured.
Submit a formal data recovery request to the backup team if shadow copy fails.
Do not write new data to the disk where files were deleted (to prevent overwrite).
Escalate to L3 storage team for enterprise backup restore.
result: Data recovery in progress — backup team engaged.
escalationReason: Backup restore requires L3 storage team involvement.

title: Webcam / Microphone Not Working
description: Camera not detected, microphone not working, video call issues, Teams/Zoom no audio
steps: Check Device Manager for webcam/microphone errors — update or rollback drivers.
Verify app permissions: Settings → Privacy → Camera / Microphone → Allow.
Test the device in a browser-based test (e.g., webcamtests.com) to isolate OS vs app issue.
Close all other apps that may be using the camera/mic.
In Teams/Zoom: Settings → Devices → select the correct audio/video device.
Plug external USB webcam/mic into a different USB port.
Restart the Windows Audio service: Services → Windows Audio → Restart.

title: Microsoft Teams Issues (Not Loading / Login Error)
description: Teams not starting, Teams frozen, Teams login loop, error code caa20004
steps: Sign out of Teams and sign back in.
Clear Teams cache: close Teams → delete contents of %appdata%\Microsoft\Teams.
Run Teams as Administrator.
Check Microsoft 365 service health dashboard for any outages.
Re-install Teams from the Microsoft website or IT portal.
Verify that Microsoft 365 license is assigned to the user in admin center.
Check proxy/firewall settings if the user is on a restricted network.
result: Teams client restored and user can sign in.

title: Phishing Email Reported
description: User received suspicious email, phishing attempt, email with malicious link
steps: Instruct the user NOT to click any links or download attachments from the email.
Ask user to forward the email as an attachment to the SOC mailbox: security@company.com.
Delete the email from the user's inbox immediately.
Check if the user clicked any links before reporting.
If links were clicked: isolate the machine and initiate security incident.
Submit the sender domain to the email gateway block list.
Issue a company-wide advisory if this is a widespread campaign.
result: Phishing email reported and sender blocked.
escalationReason: Security team must investigate any link-clicked incidents.

title: Hard Drive / SSD Failure
description: Disk read error, hard drive clicking noise, SSD not detected, storage failure
steps: Immediately run a backup of all accessible data to a network share.
Run SMART diagnostics: use CrystalDiskInfo or WMIC diskdrive.
Boot from a USB diagnostic tool to assess disk health.
If SMART reports critical failure, initiate hardware replacement immediately.
Escalate to hardware team with the SMART report attached.
Order a replacement drive via the procurement portal.
Re-image the machine with the user's backed-up data after replacement.
escalationReason: Physical hardware failure — L2 hardware team required.

title: License Expired / Activation Error (Office / Windows)
description: Microsoft Office activation error, Windows license expired, product not activated
steps: Check license assignment in Microsoft 365 Admin Center → Active Users.
Re-assign the Office or Windows license if expired or removed.
On the user's machine, run: cscript slmgr.vbs /ato to force activation.
If Office: open any Office app → File → Account → Activate Product.
Ensure the machine has internet access to reach Microsoft activation servers.
If using KMS/MAK: verify the KMS server is reachable from the machine.
Restart the machine after activation.

title: USB Device Not Recognized
description: USB drive not detected, USB device not working, external hard drive not showing
steps: Try the USB device in a different USB port.
Check Device Manager for unknown or error devices under Universal Serial Bus.
Uninstall the USB device from Device Manager and reconnect it.
Update USB host controller drivers from Device Manager.
Test the USB device on another machine to rule out device failure.
Check if USB access is restricted by company policy (GPO/DLP).
If blocked by policy: submit a data transfer request via the IT portal.

title: Remote Desktop (RDP) Connection Failed
description: Cannot RDP into a machine, Remote Desktop not working, RDP error code
steps: Verify the target machine is powered on and connected to the network.
Confirm Remote Desktop is enabled: System Properties → Remote → Allow Remote Connections.
Check Windows Firewall: ensure Remote Desktop rule is enabled (port 3389).
Verify the user is in the Remote Desktop Users group on the target machine.
Check that corporate firewall allows RDP traffic to the target subnet.
Try connecting via IP instead of hostname.
Restart Remote Desktop Services on the target machine via services.msc.

title: Server / Service Outage
description: Server is down, critical service unavailable, production outage, all users affected
steps: Immediately alert the on-call infrastructure team via the incident hotline.
Open a P1 (Critical) incident ticket in the ITSM system.
Check the server status dashboard and monitoring alerts (Nagios/Datadog).
Attempt to ping and SSH/RDP into the server for diagnostics.
Review recent change management logs for any changes made in the last 24h.
Initiate the Business Continuity/DR plan if RTO is at risk.
Send a stakeholder communication every 30 minutes until resolution.
Post-incident: conduct a Root Cause Analysis within 48 hours.
result: Incident escalated to infrastructure team. Stakeholders notified.
escalationReason: Critical P1 outage — infrastructure and management must be engaged.

title: Unauthorized Access / Privilege Escalation Attempt
description: Unauthorized login, suspicious privilege escalation, admin access attempt, security alert
steps: Lock the affected user account immediately from Active Directory.
Notify the Security Operations Center (SOC) and CISO office.
Review authentication logs in SIEM (Splunk/Sentinel) for the time window.
Identify the source IP and block it via firewall rules.
Capture all relevant logs and preserve evidence.
Initiate the Access Control Incident Response runbook.
Conduct a full permission audit on affected systems.
If insider threat is suspected, involve HR and Legal.
result: Account locked. Security investigation in progress.
escalationReason: Security incident — SOC, CISO, and potentially Legal involvement required.

title: Two-Monitor / Display Setup Issue
description: Second monitor not detected, display resolution wrong, screen flickering, HDMI/DisplayPort not working
steps: Check physical cable connection (HDMI/DisplayPort/USB-C) at both ends.
Right-click Desktop → Display Settings → Detect → check if monitor appears.
Update display/graphics drivers from Device Manager or GPU vendor site.
Try a different cable or adapter.
Set the correct display mode: Extended, Duplicate, or Second Screen Only (Win+P).
Check monitor's own input selector button — ensure correct input is selected.
Roll back recent graphics driver update if issue appeared after an update.

title: Employee Offboarding — Account Deprovisioning
description: Employee leaving, disable account, revoke access, offboarding, termination
steps: Receive approved offboarding request from HR with last working date.
On the last working day: disable the AD account (do NOT delete immediately).
Revoke all Microsoft 365 licenses and active sessions.
Remove the user from all security groups and distribution lists.
Forward email to the line manager for 30 days.
Transfer OneDrive/SharePoint data to the manager.
Revoke VPN certificates and MFA registrations.
Document in CMDB and retain account for 90 days before deletion per policy.
result: User access fully revoked per offboarding policy.
escalationReason: Requires HR sign-off and manager approval.
